title: betula × zabelii
intro: Betula × zabelii, also known as Zabel's Birch, is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between the paper birch (Betula papyrifera) and the river birch (Betula nana). The medicinal properties of Zabel's Birch are attributed to its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and alleviate symptoms of rheumatism and arthritis. Zabel's Birch can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions.
